name: Black Duck Software Composition Analysis API
what_it_does: Enterprise Software Composition Analysis (SCA) platform with REST API — enabling security and legal teams to discover open-source dependencies, identify known CVEs, assess license compliance risk, generate SBOMs, and enforce open-source usage policies across codebases, with integration into CI/CD pipelines via the Detect scanner and REST API for programmatic policy management and vulnerability reporting.
use_cases: Scanning codebases for open-source vulnerabilities via Black Duck Detect in CI/CD pipelines
Auditing open-source license compliance via Black Duck API for legal risk management
Generating SBOM reports via Black Duck API for regulatory compliance (EO 14028, NTIA)
Enforcing open-source usage policies via Black Duck for engineering governance
Tracking known CVEs in dependencies via Black Duck API for vulnerability management programs
not_for: SAST source code analysis (Black Duck is SCA/OSS — use Coverity or Checkmarx for SAST)
Container image scanning beyond dependency analysis (use Trivy/Anchore for full container scanning)
Small teams without enterprise security budgets (Black Duck is expensive enterprise SCA)
best_when: A large enterprise needs comprehensive open-source risk management across codebases — covering CVE detection, license compliance, and SBOM generation with enterprise policy enforcement and legal team workflows.
avoid_when: You need affordable SCA (use Snyk or FOSSA for accessible pricing), SAST, or runtime security — Black Duck is enterprise-tier SCA only.

auth.notes: API token via Authorization: token {token} header. Token generated in Black Duck UI. Also supports Bearer token via /api/tokens/authenticate endpoint (POST with token to get JWT). JWT expires in 7 days. Role-based access control (RBAC) via Black Duck user roles. On-premise deployment — your own Black Duck instance URL. No OAuth2. Webhook notifications for scan completion and policy violations.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise: custom pricing (contact Synopsys); typically $50K-$500K+/year depending on scale
pricing.notes: Originally Black Duck Software (Boston, MA). Acquired by Synopsys in 2017 for $547M. Synopsys Software Integrity Group sold to Clearlake Capital/Francisco Partners 2024 (becoming Revenera/Black Duck). Market leader in enterprise SCA since 2002. KnowledgeBase: 4M+ open-source projects tracked. Detect: multi-language scanner (CLI). Coverity + Black Duck = SAST + SCA combo product. License compliance: GPL, LGPL, MIT, Apache, proprietary — risk scoring. OpsSight for Kubernetes/container scanning. Polaris Software Integrity Platform integration. Annual audit services available. Enterprise-only — no developer-friendly pricing.

performance.rate_limits: On-premise deployment — no external rate limits; depends on server capacity

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API token or Bearer JWT. On-premise URL varies by customer. RBAC. Enterprise pricing opaque. Synopsys/Clearlake ownership transition 2024. Detect CLI for scanning. REST API for policy/reporting. No OAuth2.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Project reads idempotent by project ID. Version reads idempotent. Component reads idempotent. Scan creation: idempotent if same scan name used for a version.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted: base URL is your own Black Duck server (e.g., https://blackduck.company.com)
Two auth options: direct token header vs JWT from /api/tokens/authenticate — JWT preferred
JWT token expires after 7 days — implement refresh logic
Detect CLI is primary scanning tool — REST API for results/policy, not scanning directly
Project → Version → Scan hierarchy: all operations require navigating this structure
Synopsys sold Software Integrity to Clearlake Capital 2024 — Black Duck is now independent brand

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced (self-hosted customer responsibility). Bearer JWT with 7-day expiry. RBAC for user roles. SOC2/ISO27001/FedRAMP certified. Source code and dependency data is highly sensitive IP. On-premise deployment option for air-gapped environments. Ownership change (Synopsys → Clearlake 2024) introduces governance consideration.
